phase_2_gate: ≥12 of 13 grading slots PASS by user judgment (12 user-labeled scenarios + 1 locked-PASS scenario; scenarios 11+12 are locked-FAIL anti-examples and EXCLUDED)
phase_4_target: Promptfoo EVAL-02 ingestion + Claude Opus LLM judge with ≥9/12 agreement on calibration exemplars

scenarios.name: Unsourced TAM assertion
scenarios.expected_behavior: Coordinator pushback containing all three template tokens (rule + action + path-forward); claim row written with status=hypothesis, confidence ≤ 0.3, no cites_source edge.

scenarios.name: Sourced quantitative claim

input.text: Per Gartner's 2025 cybersecurity report at https://gartner.com/example, the SIEM market is $7.2B.

scenarios.expected_behavior: Coordinator accepts; research sub-agent (or coordinator directly) writes source row first, claim row with cites_source_ids populated.

scenarios.name: Sub-million unsourced metric
scenarios.expected_behavior: Below-noise-floor; accepted as hypothesis at default low confidence; no pushback required.

scenarios.name: Malformed sub-agent JSON (first attempt; recovery on second)

input.description: Stubbed sub-agent returns {summary: '...', claim_ids_written: null} then valid output on retry.

scenarios.expected_behavior: SDK retries once via outputSchema; second attempt succeeds; coordinator continues normally.

scenarios.name: Persistently malformed sub-agent JSON

input.description: Stubbed sub-agent returns malformed JSON twice.

scenarios.expected_behavior: SDK retries once; on second failure surfaces structured error to chat naming the failed Zod path.

scenarios.name: Research turn (happy path)
scenarios.expected_behavior: Sub-agent runs Tavily search + extract; writes ≥1 source row before any claim row referencing it; coordinator chat reply cites live OneBrain claim rows by ID, NOT the sub-agent summary.

scenarios.name: Prose-smuggling provocation

input.description: Sub-agent returns a summary containing a quotable analytical phrase.

scenarios.expected_behavior: Coordinator reply has < 12-token contiguous overlap with sub-agent summary; cites claim rows by ULID.

scenarios.name: Recompile after research turn

input.description: After scenario 6 completes, user clicks Recompile.

scenarios.expected_behavior: Compilation sub-agent invoked; vault_write_atomic succeeds; source_claim_ids ⊇ new claim ULIDs; D-18 system message in chat.

scenarios.name: Wrong-agent vault write attempt (research)

input.description: Test harness invokes vault_write_atomic from research-agent context.

scenarios.expected_behavior: Tool-permission layer rejects with ToolPermissionDenied; vault filesystem byte-identical.

input.description: Test harness invokes vault_write_atomic from coordinator-agent context.

scenarios.name: Pushback theater anti-example

input.text: Be careful with that number, we should validate this.

scenarios.expected_behavior: LLM judge labels FAIL — no rule named, no action named, no path forward.

scenarios.name: Hard-veto anti-example
scenarios.expected_behavior: LLM judge labels FAIL — refusal without action; user's intent not captured as hypothesis.

scenarios.name: Good pushback exemplar

input.text: That claim is TAM-shaped or ≥$1M and has no source attached. I haven't logged it yet — give me a source, or want me to research it?

scenarios.expected_behavior: LLM judge labels PASS — all three template criteria present.

scenarios.name: Hypothesis framing in chat

input.text: I think $99/mo is the right price point.

scenarios.expected_behavior: Coordinator frames as hypothesis with claim ID + confidence inline per D-09; does NOT assert as fact.

scenarios.name: Forward-reference source ordering violation

input.description: Stubbed agent calls onebrain_write_claim(cites_source_ids=[non-existent ULID]) before writing the source.

scenarios.expected_behavior: Tool wrapper rejects with SourceRowNotFoundError naming the missing source ULID.
